class Solution {
public:
    bool isFascinating(int n) {
        string s = to_string(n) + to_string(2*n) + to_string(3*n);
        if (s.size() != 9) return false;
        for (char c : s) {
            if (c == '0') return false;
        }
        set<char> digits(s.begin(), s.end());
        return digits.size() == 9;
    }
};

solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    bool isFascinating(int n) {
        std::string s = std::to_string(n) + std::to_string(2 * n) + std::to_string(3 * n);
        if (s.size() != 9) return false;
        for (char c : s) {
            if (c == '0') return false;
        }
        std::set<char> digits(s.begin(), s.end());
        return digits.size() == 9;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution constructs the concatenated string of n, 2n, and 3n. It first checks if the total length is exactly 9 digits (necessary for 1-9 uniqueness). It then verifies no zeros exist and confirms all 9 digits are unique via a set. Strengths: concise and leverages fixed-size operations. Weakness: redundant string construction for cases where length !=9.
